paper_title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
passage: Residual blocks learn a change relative to their input and include shortcut connections.
plain_explanation: Instead of forcing each group of layers to build an entirely new representation, the network can learn only the useful change and carry the original signal along a shortcut.
rewrite_strategy: Explain residual learning as learning a correction.
faithfulness_notes: Describes the mechanism without claiming shortcuts solve every deep-network optimization problem.
risk_notes: Reported gains are tied to tested architectures and vision benchmarks.

paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
passage: A Transformer applied to image patches performs strongly when pretrained on large datasets and transferred to recognition benchmarks.
plain_explanation: The authors split images into small patches and process them similarly to a sequence. With large-scale pretraining, this approach matched or exceeded strong image-classification models on the reported benchmarks.
rewrite_strategy: Connect patch sequence processing to the conditional evidence.
faithfulness_notes: Keeps the important condition that large-scale pretraining was used.
risk_notes: Do not imply the approach is data-efficient in all settings.
